steps.title: Rewrite the radical as a power
steps.content:
An eighth root is the same as raising to the $\frac{1}{8}$ power. The condition $x\gt1$ keeps the base positive, so the usual exponent rules apply directly:
$$\sqrt[8]{x^5}=(x^5)^{1/8}.$$

steps.title: Apply the power rule
steps.content:
Use $(x^a)^b=x^{ab}$, which means the exponent $5$ is multiplied by $\frac{1}{8}$:
$$(x^5)^{1/8}=x^{5/8}$$
The expression is $x^m$, so $m=\frac{5}{8}$. The answer is 5/8.
